Standardise CVR numbers

Source: R/clean_cvr.R
clean_cvr.Rd

Cleans a vector of Danish CVR numbers to the canonical 8-digit form: dashes and spaces are stripped ("12 34 56 78" -> "12345678"), and a leading "DK" VAT prefix is removed ("DK12345678" -> "12345678").

Usage

clean_cvr(x)

Arguments

x

A character vector of CVR numbers. Numeric vectors are converted with as.character() first.

Value

A character vector of the same length.

Details

Unlike clean_cpr(), seven-digit values are not zero-padded: a lost leading zero cannot be distinguished from a typo, and inventing a digit would let a malformed number slip past downstream checks such as a cvr %like% "^\\d{8}$" checkpoint. Values are returned cleaned but otherwise untouched, so malformed numbers stay visibly malformed.

See also

clean_cpr()

Examples

clean_cvr(c("DK12345678", "12 34 56 78", "12345678", NA))
#> [1] "12345678" "12345678" "12345678" NA        

# Typical use: standardise the join key on both sides
# df  |> dplyr::mutate(cvr = clean_cvr(cvr))
